string.text: That’s all the measurements for now. Thanks for the escort — and for lending me your fighters.
The lab still isn’t fully explored.

True. But I’m exhausted at this point, I need a break. I’ll report to the Institute — they’ll probably send a new team here. For now, you’re free to explore the lab on your own if you wish. Here’s your reward. Thanks for the help.
Alright then... thank you! If you ever need something, just ask.
Just give the word — we’ll help, and try not to get in your way!
Stay by the fire. %c[255,248,153,26](The squad will wait here)
Follow me. %c[255,248,153,26](The squad will follow you)
Step aside, you’re blocking the way. %c[255,248,153,26](Character is in the way)
Stay by the fire. %c[255,248,153,26](The squad will wait here)
Follow me. %c[255,248,153,26](The squad will follow you)
I think I found the PDA from your expedition. They disappeared in uncharted territory.
What? Oh God... I hoped they’d just lost contact... Well, thanks for telling me.
I’m here about that job, Professor.
Yes, yes... Listen, this is a serious matter. I wouldn’t trust it to just anyone. But I believe you can handle it.
I’m listening.
I’ve changed my mind. Let’s discuss this later.

We’ve registered strong psi-waves near the sanatorium — in the pool of the old water park. They cause hallucinations, sometimes even “people from another world.” Unscientific as it sounds... in short: ghosts.
And what exactly do you want me to do?

I need you to escort my colleague, Gloria, to the water park. She’s an IT specialist and knows our equipment. She’ll install a device that records psi-effects, while you make sure nothing happens to her. I don’t think she can handle it alone... I hope it all goes smoothly. If you succeed, expect a nice reward — and discounts on my supplies.
Understood, Professor. I’ll find her and discuss the plan.
Excellent. Take the device. She should be at the bar now — I’m sure you’ll find her.
Professor, got any work for me?

I would gladly give you something, young man! But right now, it’s vital for my assistant “Riddick” to continue his research in the local caves. If you help him out, I’ll have more work for you afterwards!
Of course! Since you’ve helped my assistant, I can now entrust you with more profitable tasks.
Hey, kid. I’m from Professor Bernada.
Uncle, next to you I might be a kid, but let’s be honest — not really.
Alright then, no problem, kid.
I’ve got a name! Glo-ri-a. Got it? And what’s yours?
Oleg. But call me Voland — feels more natural. Alright, see you at the water park. Bye.
So tell me, how’d you end up in the Zone? You’re barely more than a child.
Ugh... not in the mood to talk about it. You said you’re from the Professor. What’s this about?
Yeah. He gave me this device, said you know how it works. You need to set it up in the water park near the sanatorium. Apparently, they registered some psi-activity there.
Ooooh! I’ve heard about that! Ghosts! Hah, don’t tell me you’re afraid of ghosts?
I’m only afraid the next generation will lose their pensions.
You’re such a bore, uncle... Ugh. So what’s the plan? We heading out now?
Basically, yeah.
Great! Then you carry the device to the site, I’ll throw my stuff together and join you. See you there, okay?
Professor Bernada, mission complete. Gloria’s fine, data is intact.
You truly are my esteemed Mr. Voland! I’m very grateful. Here’s your reward, and as promised — discounts on my humble supplies. I’m glad everything worked out, and thank you for helping my colleague!
Hello, young man. How can I help you?
Professor, what are you doing here? That’s a reactor behind the wall — it looks both impressive and dangerous!
That’s right. But we monitor all parameters closely, and we’re ready to shut down the experiment at any moment.
Experiment?
Exactly. We’re testing ways to generate energy. This very reactor powers the lighting and systems in the lab.
But the radiation must be building up... what do you do with it?
We try to keep its level stable, as well as the output of this anomalous reactor.
Anomalous... so it’s not man-made?

Exactly. We’re experimenting with a shard of an artifact, similar to the well-known “Incubator.” Sadly, the artifact itself has never been found. As far as I know, Professor Sokolov searched for it, but failed.
Yeah, right. There was a stalker in their team named “Kramer.” I chased him before he was discharged — still just a kid... They say he later died on the mainland. Poor guy.
Let’s not dwell on the sad. Humanity still awaits new technologies and discoveries.
And how did you end up settling here?

Hard to explain... We came here as part of an expedition into the Zone. Underground, near the airbase, we picked up strange radiation readings. Turned out there was a hidden lab under the base. We took control of it and continued experiments with the reactor down there. Heh... Friend, we’ve been here more than ten years now. A lot has happened. Lately though, things have settled, stable enough to keep working. And that’s what matters.
Wait... you’re saying you boosted that artifact shard yourself, to this level?
Exactly. Before us, the radiation here was unstable, weak. When we studied the site, we realized the experiments began back in the ’90s. We just picked up where they left off.
And you are...? Don’t you see I’m busy— Major?! Damn, good to see you!
Naumov? What the hell... You’ve been listed dead for a year! Alive, huh? Well I’ll be damned.
Comrade Major, my apologies!
I’m Lieutenant Colonel now. Relax, I’m off duty, captain. Came here on my own. Better tell me where you vanished to... and what you’re doing in the Zone.

Well... Comrade Lieutenant Colonel, I’m off duty too. Operation “Fairway” failed. I was the only one left. Escaped... but after that, I don’t know who I am anymore. Stalker? Deserter? Worse? Freedom found me half-dead and dragged me here. That’s how it went.
I see... Good you survived. Don’t worry, captain, I won’t hand you over, haha.
And you? Why are you here, if it’s not a secret?
I’m looking for my younger brother. Thinks he can be a hero. Long story... family mess.
Understood... Sorry for getting in your way.
It’s fine.
Ah, Zone... What hole have you dragged me into now?
What do you want? Ugh... I’m sick of rotting in this can, I swear.
Hey there. Need something?
If only someone would lend a hand...
Welcome, stalker. What can I do for you?
Ha! Hey there, uncle.
Grandpa, if I may... how’d you end up in Freedom? You’re not exactly young anymore.
Heh, what’s wrong with that, boy? It’s quiet here, calm. Why not join Freedom? Scientists nearby keep things steady. No complaints.
You got a proper technician around?
Sadly... no skilled hands for the gadgets we need. Makes life harder.
What do you think of that reactor the scientists are messing with?
Honestly, son... I don’t like it. Just hope their tinkering doesn’t blow up in our faces.
Tell me about yourself.

Not much to tell... I used to be a stalker, but age caught up. Freedom offered me bar work here, said it’s warm near the reactor—hah, a bit too warm maybe. Feels like we’re soaking up rads every day... Anyway. I hunted with Lesnik in Red Forest, tracked beasts, even chased a chimera to the army depots. Barely made it back alive. Freedom patched me up, saved my skin. After that... well, I stayed. Joined their ranks, so to speak.
Keep an eye on trader Garrota. Better not let him leave the bunker until I’m done checking something, got it?
Heh, you’re a funny one... Alright, he’s not going anywhere till you say so.
You seen where Garrota ran off to?
Of course! He’s over at the scientists’ complex. Just head through the canyon, you’ll find them there.
I’m ready. Let’s head for your caves.
You sure? Place isn’t safe... Mutants could be lurking, though with all that acid down there, maybe nothing’s left alive.
So you’re set? Where’s your suit? Without it, you won’t last a second — that acid will eat you alive.
Yes. I’m fully prepared.
Hold on.
What happened to you?
I need to explore a cave under the complex. Can’t go alone... and no one else will.
I’ll help you.
We’ll talk later.

That’s good... but your suit won’t cut it. You’ll need a sealed one, or an exoskeleton at least. Otherwise, those chemicals will melt it... and then you. Don’t even think that “Zaslon” helmet will save you — maybe your lungs, but your body will dissolve in seconds. Not much of a future, eh? Get proper gear, then we’ll talk.
Really? Perfect. Looks like your suit will hold. I’ll wait until you’re ready.
We made it... still alive. Hah... Feels like the fumes in there weakened, but I wouldn’t risk taking the suit off. Let’s get back to base fast.
Let’s move.
Wait, let me check the area.
Professor Bernada, stalker Bes said you could lend me a decoder...
Decoder? And what for?
A spatial bubble opened in the settlement. We need to shut it down before it grows.
Hmm... You’re right, not something to mess with. Here, take it — I trust you.
Why are you staring at me like that? I’m not guilty, damn it...
You said you’d just returned from a mission when you came to the Zone. What kind of mission, if you don’t mind me asking?

Recently, Professor Zadorov’s expedition under Captain Smirnov reestablished contact. Their signal was jammed by some device under Yantar, until one brave stalker disabled it... they called him “Assistant”, can’t recall. Anyway, I went to extract them. Smirnov said the plan failed, and Zadorov... ran off with the artifact they were studying. Didn’t hear the rest, rotor noise drowned it out. That’s the story.
So Captain Smirnov made it back? Where is he now?
I see.
At the Research Institute, of course. Waiting for clearance... for the NPP assault, they say.
Got it. Thanks.
This might be personal... but what happened to your hand and face?
Normally, that’d be rude to ask a woman. But I’m not your “normal” woman... so I don’t mind, if you really want to know.
I’m listening.
You’re right... not my business.

I’ve got a mechanical implant instead of my right hand... and my face was burned. Operation “Fairway.” I was piloting when Monolith hit us with Gauss rifles. Helicopter was on fire, barely under control... but I landed it. Rough landing, but I lived. People from the Institute found me and patched me up.
And you still fly after that? Most would quit.
As you see, I’m not broken. Fire still haunts me... but I’ll manage.
You’re a strong one. Respect.
Heh... Thanks for saying that.
Hello... Need something?
I’ve heard of you... Looks like the Prophet’s got himself a new toy for dirty work. Why the long face? Am I wrong? Heh...
Do you like it here? The fog... the creaking trees, the whispers in the bushes... Feels like something’s about to tear into you from behind. Cute, huh?
I’ve got solid info — the cultists are planning to steal classified documents from the Institute.
Damn... I need to report this fast. Thanks!
Glad to help.
You fight well, Voland. Been watching you a while... Too bad we’re enemies.
A... Attr... Attribute!
Nikita! Snap out of it, brother!
A... A... A-t-t-r-i-b-u-t-e... Attribute!
Hey, what’s happening to you?!
Attribute... I serve... I serve the Attribute!
What have they done to you... %c[0,140,140,140][...end dialogue...]
A... Attr... Attribute! I serve the Attribute! Who’s there!? Attribute!
What do you want, sinner?
A-a... You’re my guest today? Still breathing? Strange... What questions do you bring me, sinful soul?
I need to reach your base.
No traces left, understand!? Fine... mask on. Let’s move!
Start it.
I need to get to the surface.
I’ll guide you out. Put the mask on — you won’t remember the way back here. That’s it, let’s move.
Lead the way.
Ah, my dear guest... Look at this! Isn’t it beautiful? Simply wonderful, watching your friend... Seems he couldn’t withstand my hypnosis! Ha-ha-ha.
You... Where’s my brother!?
Let my brother go!

Ha-ha-ha! Humans are cruel... But I’ll make a man out of you! Don’t raise your voice at me, hermano. I’ve dug through your head — tik-tak... and learned so much! You can be useful! And do you know the worst part? Alive! Yes... you’re useful to me alive! Ha-ha-ha! Because your dear brother... Nikita — is now under my special control. This is a test, a path for both of us! I haven’t killed him or sold him for organs yet. Coincidence? Nonsense! I knew... I knew he was worth much more! Ha-ha-ha-ha!

Have you seen him already? Eh... Humans are cruel... But I’ll make a man out of you! Don’t raise your voice at me, hermano. I’ve dug through your head — tik-tak... and learned so much! You can be useful! And do you know the worst part? Alive! Yes... you’re useful to me alive! Ha-ha-ha! Because your dear brother... Nikita — is now under my special control. This is a test, a path for both of us! I haven’t killed him or sold him for organs yet. Coincidence? Nonsense! I knew... I knew he was worth much more! Ha-ha-ha-ha!
You’re insane! Where is he!?
You’re completely mad!
Look there, in that cell. I’ll wait. Admire him, then return quickly, my dear friend! We have matters to discuss! Ha-ha-ha!
Me? Crazy? Ha-ha-ha... No, psychos are humans, broken humans. But I’m not crazy, my friend. It’s the people around us — they’re wrong. You understand, hermano?
If even one hair on his head... %c[0,140,140,140][...end dialogue...]
I need to make sure he’s alright! %c[0,140,140,140][...end dialogue...]
Where are my things?
Your things? Ha-ha-ha... They’re waiting for you! Ha-ha-ha.
Stop laughing. I can’t do your tasks without them.
You’re right, Voland, you’re absolutely right! Then I permit you to take them. They’re right here, behind me, in this... beautiful container. Go on, take them!
Need any help, Voland?
Glad to see familiar faces! Ho-ho... How’s life?
Well, well... Look who it is! Voland! Back from vacation already?
Ugh, I’m sick of this institute already... Maybe I should head to the Zone. What do you need, Voland?
I came for the decoder.
Yes, yes... Here you go, Voland! All yours!
Don't start with me.
Ha, alright, alright... everything’s fine.
Greetings, Voland. Need repairs or an upgrade? I can handle it—any whim, for the right price!
Hello, hello. Business or pleasure?
I need to get to the Zone. We flying?
Alright then, to the Zone... let’s go.
Let’s move out.
Hold on, I forgot something.
No problem, but we’ll only fly once the emission passes.
Got it.
No offense, old man, but I’m not in the mood to talk right now.
Well, hello there, old friend.
Voland?! Old man, good to see you!
Cap! Buddy! Look who it is!
Heard you ditched your vacation, searching for your brother... How’s that going?
Mm... Complicated, my friend. He’s a fool—young, naive, green... you get me.
I get it... but youth can surprise you. I’ve seen it myself.
Who’s this rascal? Judging by your face, you mean one of your men, right?

Ha, you could say that. We had a kid with us on expedition—an assistant to the professor. "Assistant" became his nickname, heh. But this kid blew us all away—turned into a real monster of a soldier! Saved my life in the lab under Yantar, too. I went in alone, told him to stay put in the bunker, but he came after me... If not for him, I’d still be rotting there as a posthumous captain. And the way he changed since then... Once jumpy and nervous, now probably wrestles mutants barehanded. But his eyes... that stare—chills you to the bone. I saw huge potential in him, but I fear he’ll lose his mind. Maybe I shouldn’t have let him stay in the Zone. He’s dead set on finding that runaway professor... Another madman. So here I sit, waiting for command to greenlight my trip to Limansk to track that bastard down. I just hope I cross paths with Assistant along the way—alive and in one piece. Shouldn’t have left him... Guess I got too attached, huh?
Going soft, old soldier? Just kidding. We all mellow with age, no matter what life’s thrown at us...
That’s true, Voland... So, what are your plans?
If only I knew, buddy... If only I knew.
Hello, Voland... What brings you here?
Damn... what a mess...
Greetings, soldier! How are you holding up?
Hey there, uncle Voland. How’s life treating you?
Greetings!
Yes? Mr. Voland, do you have some questions for me?
I’ve got a relic from the past—an old blueprint for item No. 62.
Whoa! That’s practically a rarity! You brought it to me, I hope?
Indeed. To you. From what I know, you’re the one with the most downtime in this institute.
Ha! Time ticks, the paycheck’s safe, and I hope they never think of cutting down the tech staff. Otherwise… back to the Zone, and I’d rather not go. Hehe...
Here, take the blueprint—have fun with it.

Thanks a lot, Voland. Don’t go yet! Between us... ahem… I’ve got a few interesting suits lying around. Since they’d just gather dust, I want you to have one. Look at this… a Monolith exoskeleton. Not sure what the fanatics were thinking, but their suits were top-notch. Here, take it!
Wow, amazing gift. Where did you get it?
Assistant brought it from Chernobyl NPP. He collected a lot of unusual stuff, but I kept this one for you.
Could I borrow a decoder?
For someone like you, of course, Mr. Voland! But tell me—out of curiosity—what’s it for, if you don’t mind sharing?
A spatial bubble appeared in the settlement, and I need to close it. Ahem… What would I owe you for helping?

You owe nothing! A spatial bubble, you say? Dangerous Zone phenomenon, indeed! We studied something similar near Jupiter once. Deactivate it quickly! Go to Phobos’ warehouse—he’ll be expecting you, and he’ll hand over the device.
Thanks for your help, Professor.
Always glad to help!
Hello, soldier. Looking to buy something, or is there business with me?
I’ve found two fighters for your team.
Excellent! Now only the small matter remains—we’ll head to the factory soon. Are you coming?
Yes, it’s safer with me along, hehe.
Good. Once you’re ready, let me know and we’ll move out.
Greetings, soldier! Could you lend me a hand?
What’s the problem?
Putting together a team for my expedition. Eh.
I’m on it, madam.
Good. I’ll wait to hear about the team’s progress.
Uh-huh.
Well, spill it—what happened?
Busy right now. We’ll talk later.

I’m assembling a team to explore an underground lab beneath the factory. We just discovered it. I know roughly where the emergency entrance is. I can cover preparation and ammo costs, but no one else wants to go there.
Hmm… probably scared.
Most likely…
Let me help. What do you need from me?
Thanks! First, I need a couple more people for reliability. Anyone come to mind?
I’ll ask around. Maybe someone will step up.
I'm ready for the expedition.
Are you sure? All set?
Yes, quite ready.
Hold on a second.
Good. Then we’re heading out now. Here, take this—an advance for you!
Excellent. Let’s move.
I wanted to ask… Would you join Athena’s team? She’s gathering people for an expedition to an underground lab under the industrial factory.
Sounds interesting! I’m in. Where do I go?
Just like that? Well, alright. She’s waiting at the stalkers’ camp near the “fish farm” base.
Got it. I’ll grab my stuff and head there. See you soon!
That was easy…
Athena is assembling an expedition to explore an underground lab under the industrial factory. She’s covering all costs and paying well for escort and protection. Want to join?
Nah, I’ll pass.
No thanks, got my own business. Maybe ask the local adventurers. Stalker Lynx likes these events, you could ask him.
No thanks, busy with my own stuff. Ask Docent Roma at the scientists’ camp by the gas station—he enjoys these expeditions.
Sorry friend, not for me. Too scary!
Uh… no, sorry. Ask someone else.
I’d join, but busy with my own things. Sorry!
Hey, want to join Athena’s team? She’s forming a group for an expedition to the underground lab under the industrial factory.

Hmm… I’d like to, but there’s a rare mutant bugging me! Shot it in the swamps, but the bastard crawled off… Scientists promised a big reward for this rarity. Can’t leave it like that. Set a trap, but it escaped—no worries, it’ll crawl back!
Understood.
Excellent.
Yes! You found my wonder-fish! Now I’m rich, hehe… I’m ready to help Athena, no problem.
Found your wonder-fish. Didn’t crawl far—belly up, just ten meters from here.
Ah? Really? Damn, I’m blind! Thought I checked everything… Thanks, buddy! Here, you earned it!
Why the long face? What are you catching?
Catching, yeah… a wonder-fish!
Tell me more.
We’ll talk later.
Hunting a rare local fish—Rotan. Shot it, but it dashed into the bushes… Can’t find it. Probably escaped. Sitting, waiting for it to come back. Set a trap. They’ll pay well for this one… Eh…
Is there work?
Ho-ho-ho! Of course! Found a mushroom field recently. Not ordinary—anomalous mushrooms! Wonder what they taste like, huh? Hehe…
Well, tell me, storyteller.
No, I’ll save the details for now.

Ha! Knew my story would catch your attention… Walked from the trader at night, near the Engineers’ district—saw light far away. Investigated—big glowing mushroom field! Binoculars in hand, it’s a pity it was night… Chickened out alone. Now, thinking… should I check it? Want to come along?
Hmm… Let’s do it! Curious myself.
Super! Meet here at 2 AM.
Shall we check your field?
Yes, of course! Let’s go!
Ah? I said 2 AM! Nothing to see now…
Alright, let’s go.
Okay.
You scared me, Voland! Thought you were done for…
Me? You were in delirium yourself. Woke up before me, I guess?
If only… Woke up here, saw you opposite me. And that’s not all…
I don’t get it. What do you mean?
We’re trapped. Tried leaving this room… teleport… nothing.
You didn’t drag me here, and we’re locked in this room, right?
Yes… Someone locked us in. Connection’s gone. Tried signaling the others… only interference.
Bad! Let me look around.
We’re stuck… What happened?
Probably inhaled those mushrooms…
Seems so. Now trapped, no connection… What a trap, ugh.
Wait, we’ll think of something.
We’ll figure something out.
Maybe it’s an illusion… maybe we’re still asleep?
I’d like to believe that…
We’ll figure something out.
What mushrooms are these, anyway?
Who knows! But scientists will definitely be interested. Main thing is, we need to get out of here.
Yeah…
Thanks for the help, Voland. Would’ve been scary alone here. Here’s your share, hehe.
Wait… Doesn’t it bother you that we were trapped in an anomaly and couldn’t get out?
What… I said 2 AM! Nothing to see there now…
Nonsense. Those weren’t hallucinations, I’m sure.
Uh… You look rough. Buddy, let’s head back to camp—who knows what’s going on with you.
You’re right… Let’s go.
You go ahead, I need to check around here.
Greetings, soldier. Need something?
Doc, do you know what this "Chemical Clot" is?
Some anomalous formation, apparently… Take it to the scientists at the gas station. They should know what to do.
What are these ultraviolet lamps for in the camp?

Ah… Scientists gave them to us. They help preserve the nervous system during emissions. Really works! If you’re near one during an emission, even outside, your chances of survival go up. Scientists even carry them in case of sudden emissions. Really handy.
Your dog?
Yes. Came wounded to our camp, I treated her and she stayed.
Got it.
I’d like to buy some meds.
Glad to help, Voland. But trade is tricky at the moment…
Can I help somehow?
I need someone to hire a new courier to bring me medications…
I’ll help you, Doc.
Really? You’d do that, friend? I know some at the local scientific base… Ask if there’s an assistant free to help with medication supply to the base.
No problem.
Later then.
Thanks, I appreciate it!
That’s it, Doc. Found you a courier. Have you met?
Thanks a lot, Voland! Already knew him, good kid. Medication issues solved—if you need something, you’re welcome.
Greetings, Voland. What brings you here?
Professor, can you study this "Chemical Clot"?
Let me see… I’ll need time. Seems it’s not even an artifact… Leave it with me, I’ll study it and update you once I have results.
Understood, Professor. Thanks.
Do you happen to know what this "Chemical Clot" is?
Uh… no idea, friend. Sorry!
No clue, bro. Better ask the scientists.
Artifact or something? Who knows, man. Go ask the ecologists—they might know.
Don’t know… probably some artifact.
Take this "thing" to the scientists at the gas station. They should help you out!
Thanks for getting me out… I owe you one with my guys at the camp. If not for you, who knows what those sectarians would’ve done… Heard they trade organs, ugh…
Hello. Any questions? Or need your gear fixed?
Greetings, comrade Voland. What brings you here?
M? What do you want?
%c[0,140,140,140]I need medical help.
Let me check you now… hmm-hmm, yes…
All done. You should feel better!
Hmm… Voland, you’re fine. Maybe just a cold?
Heard about Doc? He’s looking for free couriers—short on people for delivering meds.

Well… I’m short on staff too. Hmm… Wait! Ask stalker "Rezak"—maybe he’ll agree. But I can’t just let him go… He’s under contract with us and supposed to deliver an artifact. He’s dragging his feet. Talk to him—if he completes it, I could transfer him to your Doc.
Understood, Professor.
Hello. Any questions for me?
Professor, what exactly do these ultraviolet lamps do?

Ah, hehe… We made these with the Research Institute. They protect the nervous system during psi-strikes of emissions. You can survive even in the open during eruptions thanks to these. Useful, though expensive. We hope to make them lighter and more compact soon for easier personal use. Mass production is next.
Where should I look for that artifact? Any clues?
Eh… Only thing I know is near electrical anomalies. That’s as far as I got… m-da.
Hello, how can I help?
Hello, "Rezak", right?
Yeah, that’s me. What’s up?
Doc wants a courier. He’s short on people to deliver medications.
Eh… I’d help Doc. He helped me once. But I’m tied up. Contract with scientists, have to deliver the "Compass" artifact. Don’t even know where to look… Sitting around, wearing out my pants. Ahem…
Got it. I could try helping. If I find it, you settle with the scientists and become Doc’s courier. Deal?
Ha! Deal, of course.
Great.
Found your "Compass". Terms still stand?
Thanks! Of course. I’ll hand it to the professor and head to the Fish Farm base to help Doc!
Excellent.
What have you learned about this clot?
An anomalous formation, hard to call an artifact. Probably a weird chemical reaction. No practical use, but fascinating. If you don’t mind, I’ll keep it in my collection and give you a small reward.
Alright… good.
Whoa! And you’re a buddy of this "blue" guy, huh?
Who-who?
Leaving… leaving.
Out of the way, small fry.
Are you crazy?! Empty your pockets, now!
Sorry, the devil tempted me. Take it—all I got.
You’re done, chickens!
Watch your mouth, uncle. Alright, leave now!
Yes sir.
The mercenary hanging here said someone would come to ransom him, ha!
Move, or I’ll lay you all down!
Don’t know who you mean.
Too brave, huh? Empty pockets and on your knees, girl.
Yeah? Whatever. Empty your pockets, bro.
You’ll answer for calling me a girl now!
Alright. Here, take everything.
Good, you didn’t start trouble. Now leave.
My bad, guys—sorry!
Uh-huh.
You goats crazy? Move, trash.
Too late, uncle. You’re done! Haha… Alright, I’m kind today. Empty your pockets and go.
What did you say?! Hey, bros, he doesn’t respect us.
Alright, don’t freak out.
Calm! Here, take it—all I got.
Pray. Face down, snot-noses!
Oh, just like that from the start. Fine, now get out of here.
Ugh… Got set up… M-m… don’t have long left.
Damn… They hit you hard. Need to drag you to a medic. A medkit won’t cut it here.
Doesn’t matter… won’t make it to a medic anyway. Listen, I have a dying request… hah… cough-cough…
Well, tell me.
You’re heading to our bar, right?
How did you guess?
Hah… Would you be killing those bastards if you didn’t need something here? Cough… Got a pass at least?
Pass? No. I’m on trader business with Garrota. Maybe you know him…
M-m… Yeah, I know him. Cough… So, since you’re going to us: avenge me, will you? Send the rat who caused this to the other side with me. Cough-cough…
Tell me who he is, I’ll handle it.

Marcus… His nickname: Marcus… Cough-cough… Get rid of him, he’s probably living it up in the bar off me. Take the reward yourself. He took my loot, led to bandits… and now I’ll die here like a dog… Cough-cough… That’s it… can’t take anymore… Bye.
I’ll avenge you… don’t worry. Good night, fighter.
Give you a medkit? Maybe it’ll ease the pain…
Joking? Nothing will save me now… Listen, I have a dying request… hah… cough-cough…
Diesel, can you open this container? %c[255,248,153,26](Closed "Freedom" container)
Friend… I’d help, but too lazy right now. Ask the ecologist in camp—maybe he’ll do it!
Could you open this container? %c[255,248,153,26](Closed "Freedom" container)
Show me… Haha, nothing complicated. Don’t move, I’ll handle it… Done! Wow, solid arsenal here. You’re lucky, take it all!
Phobos, could you open this container? %c[255,248,153,26](Closed "Freedom" container)
Easy! For this, I’ll take 7,000 rubles. Deal?
Alright, go ahead.
Changed my mind.
Excellent! Done! Looks like solid loot—take your goods!
Technician, open this container? %c[255,248,153,26](Closed "Freedom" container)
Hmm… Wasting tools is a pity… but for 5,000 I’ll do it.
Deal. Here.
Will come back later.
Don’t go far… Done! Good loot for such a price. Lucky you, heh.
Screw, open this container? %c[255,248,153,26](Closed "Freedom" container)
Show me. Pff… Piece of cake! I’ll take 2,000 rubles for consumables.
Excellent. Here.
We'll discuss it later.
Here we go. Op-pa! Damn, that’s some good loot! Lucky-lucky!
Prophet, I have information. Your man wants to remove you from your "duties".
Hermano! Surprised! Ha-ha-ha, joke! But… Are you serious? Is it true? And who dares to be insolent?
Quantum.
Of course, just a joke. Who would dare mess with you in your "community"…
M-mm… I suspected this scoundrel… I’ll make sure he sleeps very sweetly. And you’re smart, Voland! High five, ha-ha-ha! Now go, you actually have a very important task, if you haven’t forgotten!
Uh-huh.
Do you hear that too?!
Well, yes…
Suddenly it got scary. What will you do?
And what should I do? Wait for your scanner to record all this.
Maybe check what’s happening there?
Is that really necessary?
I don’t know… But I wouldn’t go there.
Watch the device. I’ll check what’s up there.
Hello! Always glad to see new visitors! Come in-come in, go down the corridor, changing rooms to your right! Quickly put on your swimsuits and go swimming!
Is everything giggles and laughs for you?
I think I already said you’re a bore, uncle Oleg!
Here, take the device. Let’s do the assigned task.
Yes-yes-yes. No problem. Starting already…
Forward and with a song!
Ugh… Who’s here?!
Quiet-quiet, Gloria. Everything’s fine, it’s me—Oleg. How do you feel?
They’re here… They’re dangerous!
Calm down, kid. Get up, we need to get out of here.
No! It will return! Uncle Oleg, stop it!
Stop what? Gloria, come on, get up. We need to leave.
No! I won’t go back! And where’s the device?
I don’t know. Wanted to ask you. Stop fooling around, come on!
Get the device back, uncle Oleg! Professor will tear my head off!
Ugh… Come to your senses. I’ll find the device, I will.
So what? Did you find the device?
Yes. Check, is it intact?
Let’s see… Lucky, undamaged. Scanning data is fine! Professor Bernada will be calm.
Yeah… We really got into a mess.
Main thing is everything worked out, uncle Oleg. Thank you so much for not leaving me.
Where would I… Do I look like a bad uncle?
Well… Nah! You’re one of the few better than you seem! Hehe.
Glad for you. Job done—means we can leave?
Yes. I’ll head to the complex now. You with me or later?
Yes, let’s go.
You go, carefully. I’ll drop by the professor later.
Do you need this medallion?
Show me… Nah, that’s sectarian stuff. Hanging on psychos’ necks. No-no-no, put it away.
Ew, put it away. That’s satanic crap!
Beh, only Danya Bely from the stalkers' camp might buy such crap from you, I'm sure...
What is it? Ooo… No thanks! That's a sectarian trinket—it won't bring any good. Better throw it away!
And what are these ultraviolet lamps in the camp for?
These devices help survive the emission, even in the open… Science is awesome, right?
Molag, got something to talk about. I made a deal with Garrota. He has the key-card, but in return, I have to do his task.
Ugh… Better if he took money. But, it is what it is. And what's the task?
He asked to deliver a case to a mercenary. Does the nickname "Cleaver" mean anything to you?
Hmm… Heard about that thug. He’s like the leader in the local bar. So he’s sending you there?
Yes. Marked their "VIP entrance" for me. It's not far…
I don't know what to advise… Heard a lot of bad about this mercenary. Don’t get into trouble! Let me follow, cover your back.

I don’t recommend it. I think that way we’ll only provoke conflict. I need everything to go quietly. Bring the case, take the envelope, give it to the trader. If things get hot, I’ll send SOS to your PDA. Then save me, hehe.
As you say, Voland. As you say…
I need to go to the Research Institute. Can you drop me there?
Let's fly, no problem.
Forward.
I changed my mind. Wait.
We'll fly, no problem. But only after the emission.
Good.
Listen, soldier… Not now. I'm not in the mood to talk, okay.
Do you happen to have a decoder?
Alas, Mr. Voland. For such equipment, you should contact the Research Institute.
Sheriff. Here's the thing: I made a deal with Garrota. He has the key-card, but in return, I have to work for him.
Details? What is he up to now…
I need to deliver a case to a local mercenary nicknamed "Cleaver". Know any?

Uh-huh. Former soldier, neighboring unit near the Research Institute—served as battalion commander for 20 years. I'm familiar. Haven’t been in contact for a long time since this killer disappeared. Now he’s a “big shot” in the Zone. I smell danger. Let me and Molag go with you?
No. Better stay here. If we ruin the deal because of extra staff, I won't get the key-card. If danger comes, I’ll send SOS. Then save me, hehe.
You're thinking right, Voland. Good, stay healthy. If anything—we’ll fly in.
Do you know a trader nicknamed Garrota? Where could he have gone?
Everyone knows this trader. Hmm… Maybe he ditched to the Cordon again, he does that often when pressed. Very slippery type…
I think I saw him recently… Sorry, can’t be sure.
Oh, bro, I don’t even know.
Ah, it’s you… If you want to trade, come to my "shop"—my goods are there.
I feel uneasy here…
I need to go to the settlement, to the old church.
No problem. Shall we sail?
Yes. Let’s sail.
Wait a bit.
Do you happen to need some junk for recycling?

Yes. See the barrel behind me? Throw unnecessary junk there. But don’t throw weapons, I have enough of those parts! I need pistol, electronic, and rifle parts, disassembled, of course! Armor is useful for fabric, will remove plates from helmets too! Hah, you can even leave artifacts if pockets are tight, and some small parts, might come in handy… But don’t throw guns, they’ll just lie there. I’ll dig through and take what I need. Won’t give much money, but you’ll get rid of junk.
Well, did you pick something from the junk I left in your barrel?
Yes, here’s the money.
The spatial bubble is gone.

Good. Thanks for not standing aside and dealing with this phenomenon. Who knows how fast it could have grown and wiped out everything around… Though part of the credit is yours—you fixed it, so you earned the reward! On behalf of all free stalkers, here’s what you deserve! Hehe…
Just wonderful!
Did you see the spatial bubble near the tower?
Of course I saw it! Hard to miss… Something tells me you’re involved, right?

Apparently. I found an unmanned scout in the river that disappeared before my eyes. But it left a memory module, which thanks to Lyosha we unlocked. Got a couple of bearings leading to local locators. Readings showed some noise… So yeah, I probably triggered this bubble.
I see… Weren’t the strange things in the "uncharted territories" enough? Now this will happen here too.
What do you mean?
Such bubbles are dangerous. Time stops in them and people disappear… And not just people, they can swallow entire villages! Do you know how many landmarks have vanished because of these things?
I’ve heard bits and pieces…
Exactly. We need to get rid of it before it grows and devours our camp.
And how do we do that?
With a decoder. Professor Bernada once had a few specimens, but I don’t know if they still exist—or even work…
The institute probably has similar gadgets too.
I hope so. We need to use it quickly. No telling how fast this bubble will grow…
I’ll figure something out.
Could you unlock the memory module?
Holy crap! I can, definitely. But… where did you find it?
It was inside a drone in the river. I tried to examine it, but it vanished before my eyes. In its place was this memory module—the carrier itself.

Brr… Creepy stuff. I’ve had enough of such tricks in the uncharted territories. But the memory module is real… We both see it, feel it. Alright, I’ll need a couple of hours to unlock it. I’ll let you know when it’s ready.
So, what’s up with the module?
It’s hacked. Here, take it. Check the info yourself.
Alright, let me see.
%c[0,140,140,140]I need medical help.
Let me see… Not fatal. You’ll live.
There! All done, good as new.
Voland, stop faking ailments, okay? Thoughts are material!
Hello, traveler!
What are you doing here?
Oh, just… Being nostalgic. And you? What brings you here?
Oh… You’re sitting up here like a hermit.

Ha! I’m not a hermit, I’m a musician! My ballads bored everyone, so they asked me to "shut up", but I can’t. I’m creative! I climbed up here to not disturb anyone. Eh… maybe tell me about yourself? Your exploits?! I’ll make poems about you!
Mm… Some other time.
So, what did you find out about the "Dream Catcher"?

Your sectarians are indeed responsible for this shard. There were several shards… I don’t know how they got them from under the NPP debris or how they discovered them, but they knew the properties and likely used them as a trap. The second shard is now in Chernobyl, at an industrial plant. Sectarians plan a ritual there.
As I recall, you told the scientists that these shards possess intelligence? Even though the artifact itself was destroyed…

Something like that… You can break it into thousands of stones, but the "brain" remains whole. Size only determines power. Now it’s weaker, maybe not aware yet, which is good. We need to destroy the second shard urgently before it grows stronger.
Need help?

You'll help, but later. Here are the coordinates. We'll meet at night. Going there during the day is impossible—sectarians will likely be guarding the place. Meanwhile, take care of your business, old man.
Call me old man again and I'll break your jaw, you little brat.
Ha! I'm terrified! Don't take it personally, Voland. You can't be offended by the truth. I'll be waiting at the place I indicated, over and out.
Greetings, traveler. Looking for quality goods? You can buy anything you desire from me, just a matter of price!
Why didn't you leave with Kvant?
He has his business, I have mine… We won’t see life with the sectarians anymore, so I have nowhere else to go.
I could lock you up in prison for this.
I know, Voland. That’s a bit unfair. After all, I was looking after your people… And everyone here could be in prison, since almost all in the Zone are unregistered. Are you going to catch them all?
So why did you stay? Do you think the stalker community will accept you?
I don’t care about their opinion. At least I’m good at making field medicine, extracting bullets, stitching wounds. A new camp here could use a medic. That’s why I’m here.
I hope you won’t cause trouble.
How are you, Artyom?
As you can see, I’ve been patched up… She patched me up. That woman… a psychopath, I swear. I wouldn’t want her among the stalkers. Well, we’ll see how she behaves.
I’m glad you’re okay. Thanks for trusting me and providing compromising info on the sectarians.

True, but the compromising material is lost. Still, Kvant isn’t stupid—most of what I collected will end up at the research institute. In a way, it worked out. Sectarians are done for; it’s only a matter of time.
That’s good to hear.
Sheriff, buddy… glad you’re alive and in one piece.
Thanks to you, Voland. Thank you for pulling me out, Nikita…
It’s a pity about Molag…
Yes. So damn young. He was a great guy… I involved him in this, he died because of me.
I’m no less to blame. Don’t shoulder it all alone, friend. What happened, happened… unfortunately.
Yes. Drop by sometime, we’ll at least commemorate him properly…
Definitely.
My respects, Voland!
Do we know each other?
By reputation. I’ve already heard about you…
What did you want?
I’m a local sailor. If you want to reach the "Rybkhoz" base in Chernobyl, I can give you a ride. I have my own "boat".
What? A working ship?
Of course! Want to check? Let’s sail now!
Good to know.
Hey there! Decided to use my "pearl"?
I need to get to the "Rybkhoz" base.
No problem. Shall we sail?
Yes. Let’s go.
Wait a bit.
Hey, dead man. What the hell are you doing here?
I need to see your "boss". I have business with him.
I wandered here by accident. I’m leaving. Calm down.
What the fuck? You’re confused, bro! And what’s his name, huh? Get out!
Shut up, scum!
I need to see Jackal. He knows me.
Got it, I’m leaving.
Huh? How do you know his nickname?! Well, come on, but no funny business. One wrong move and you’re dead. Got it?
Got it. Understood.
Shut up, asshole. You’re screwed!
What do you want, dead man?
Blow my eyes out! The prey came to me itself... I can't stop laughing, ha!
The Prophet ordered my death from you...
Ha! That's right, Voland. You're either a fool or immortal—coming to me personally, knowing we'll kill you.
No other options available?
Why not? For your head, the Prophet promised us a hundred. You could get ahead and give us two hundred. Business, baby.
Go fuck yourself!
Let's say. Here's 200 pieces. You won't hunt me anymore?
I don't have that kind of money.
Listen, I thought you'd act tough... Hehe. Walk away, soldier. I'll tell the Prophet I didn't see you. Get out of here—I'm feeling kind today.
Yeah? Then I'll have to kill you, sorry! Ha-ha!
Yeah. Like hell!
Tesak said you're a sellout scum.
Mm... So you know Tesak? Ha! That changes nothing. I don't give a fuck what he told you. Now, get ready to die, Voland.
Ladies first!
Blow my eyes out! The prey came to me itself... I can't stop laughing, ha!
The Prophet ordered my death from you... How long have you been working with the sectarians?
Ha! A long time, soldier Voland. By the way, I find it amusing to talk to a corpse! You're either a fool or immortal—coming to me personally, knowing we'll kill you.
And no other options available?
Hmm... Why not? For your head, the Prophet promised us a hundred. You could give us two hundred—business, baby.
Here's 200 pieces. Am I free now?
I don't have that kind of money.
You're finished, Jackal!
Oh, excellent! Thought you'd act tough... Hehe. Walk away, soldier. I'll tell the Prophet I didn't see you. Get out—I'm feeling generous today.
That's the problem, bro. You could have lived to retirement... Goodbye, soldier!
Tesak said you're a sellout scum.
Uh-huh...
I won't surrender without a fight.
Mm... So you know Tesak? Ha! Doesn't matter. I don't care what he said. Now, get ready to die, Voland.
Ladies first!
Tesak... A mercenary squad attacked me in the canyon. What do you have to say about it?

Impossible, Voland! My people are under my control. If any gang went after you, it’s Jackal's scum. I'm sure it was his doing. That rat works for the sectarians—their base is at the old military complex near Zalesye. I'd bet my tooth on it.
They work for the sectarians?!
You bet. Sectarians are real businessmen, damn them. Jackal gets a cut from some contracts. But why they hunted you… Holy crap… Did you really piss off those fanatics?
Mm… Got it, thanks for the info.
What, huh? I'm not in the mood.
I have mercenary "Reker's" PDA. Judging by it, you set him up.
Quiet, your fucking… Man, not here. We can make a deal: you give me the PDA, I give you 30 thousand units. Pretty good, hm?
Screw you. You set up your own man. I’ll report everything to your leader.
Deal.
I have connections, got it!? You won't get away with this!
There, you can see a businessman right away. Hehe… Now let's split up, okay? We don’t need to be seen together. Come on, get going.
I need to think about it.
I have proof that your man Markus betrayed his partner.
What! What do you mean?
Here's the kid's PDA. His name was Reker.

Let me read… Well, Markus turned out to be such scum. Umgh… And we considered him a good fighter! But we don’t need such rats in our squad. Thanks for your help, Voland. Here's your well-earned reward for not siding with that bastard.
Hm? Yeah, man. Listening with all my soul… Wanted something?
Oh, it’s you… How are you? Want something from me?
Get out of my sight, asshole. We'll meet again.
I hope you're here on business… I’m listening.
What do you want? Need something?
I'm not in the mood for talking right now. Go your way.
Holy shit! Here you are, asshole. What the hell are you doing here, scum? You're on my territory and I told you we'd meet again. Remember how you punched me in the face!?
I can do it again.
You bastard… Well, we’ll meet again—the world is round, remember that.
Well, sorry, I was heated then…
Apologies not accepted, sucker. See you later, watch your back.
Whatever you say.
I'll be waiting for our next meeting.
Oh… You're here too? How did you end up here?
Garrota, whom you were guarding, sent me here.
So that’s how it is… I see. Well, I won’t distract you. Go where you were going. Maybe we’ll see each other at the bar sometime, have a chat. Hehe…
Good. No problem.
Hello…
Where are we going?
To Tesak. They let me through.
Yes, I know. Will you find the way yourself or should I escort you?
I'll find it myself.
Well, escort me, I don’t mind.
Hello, wanderer! What can I get you? Something to eat or drink?
Can I be of help? If not, I’ll continue… Um, if you need medical supplies, I can sell some.
Voland, what business brings you here…
Mm? Need something upgraded or repaired? Or just here to chat…
Oh wow… You have business with me? Or maybe want to buy something? That’s fine, my assortment is excellent!
